analysis_description: Round 3 tape days 0–2: Black–Scholes implied vol from voucher mid and VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT wall mid; natural cubic spline IV vs log(K); leave-one-out IV prediction RMSE; best bid/ask spread stats; correlation of Δ(underlying wall mid) vs Δ(IV at strike nearest S) across subsampled timestamps. TTE: from round3work/round3description.txt, tape CSV day index d (0,1,2 in prices_round_3_day_d.csv) maps to TTE = (8−d) days; year fraction T = TTE_days/365.25. BS: r=0, no dividend.
used_to_decide: Confirmed spline interpolates observed IV smile tightly in-sample; LOO RMSE ~0.75–1.37 vol pts shows cross-strike interpolation noise (worse on day 0). Spread median 2 with mean ~5 suggests penny strikes need tighter absolute edges than deep ITM. Weak positive corr(ΔS, ΔIV_atm) on later tape days steered light extract quote skew vs voucher delta in the trader.

analysis_description: Per-strike spline-edge + Greek analysis on ROUND_3 days 0-2. For each timestamp: fit robust cubic spline IV(logK) from BS implied vols (S from VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT wall mid; TTE = 8-d days from round3description). Compute fair price via BS(spline IV), buy_edge=fair-ask1, sell_edge=bid1-fair, vega, and vega*iv_error. Summarize hit-rates and edge magnitudes by strike.
used_to_decide: Observed persistent positive edge primarily in VEV_4000 (buy_hit_rate ~0.004 with very large edge when present), while other strikes showed near-zero/negative top-of-book edge after spread in this backtester. This motivated v3/v4 to keep the same spline-IV thesis but add edge-gated multi-level taking and faster inventory recycling, with extra aggression only for VEV_4000.

analysis_description: Design note for v5/v6 (no new CSV): after v3/v4 underperformed v1, parent was reset to best PnL trader_v1. Hypothesis: BS vega weights how much 1 tick of mid move in price space is worth in IV space; combine with observed bid-ask width (spread ticks) to modulate required edge—tighter edge when |vega| is large and spread is narrow. Small grid: VEGA_EDGE_SCALE 0.15 (v5) vs 0.25 (v6). TTE unchanged: 8−d days per round3description.
used_to_decide: v5 and v6 beat v1 in this backtest (50,261 vs 50,222). v5 and v6 tie on PnL; prefer v5 (smaller scale) per tie-break. v3/v4 multi-level aggressor path still recorded as worse than v1 here, so not pursued further without new evidence.
